name: Surge
description: Simple, single-command static site publishing for front-end developers via CLI

description.value: Simple, single-command static site publishing for front-end developers via CLI
description.comment: Surge focuses on zero-configuration CLI deployment of static HTML/CSS/JS to its own CDN

parent-company.comment: Founded by Brock Whitten and Rob Ellis in Vancouver, Canada. They also co-created Apache Cordova/PhoneGap

year-launched.comment: GitHub repository created July 26, 2014; domain registered July 25, 2014

open-source.comment: The CLI tool is open source (ISC license) but the core platform/CDN infrastructure is proprietary

primary-focus.comment: Surge is purely focused on static site publishing with no JAMstack build pipeline or server-side features

free-tier.comment: Free tier includes unlimited publishing, custom domains, and basic SSL on surge.sh subdomains

free-tier-bandwidth.comment: No stated bandwidth limit on the free tier. Surge has said everything free today will remain free

free-tier-build-minutes.comment: Surge has no build system; it only uploads pre-built static files

free-tier-sites.comment: Unlimited publishing and custom domains on the free tier

paid-plan-starting-price.comment: Surge Professional at $30/month adds custom SSL, HTTPS redirect, CORS, custom redirects, and password protection

bandwidth-overage-cost.comment: No stated bandwidth limits or overage charges

enterprise-pricing.comment: Surge only offers Free and Professional ($30/month) plans; no enterprise tier is listed

git-integration.comment: Surge has no built-in Git provider integration. Deployment is done via CLI only. However, users can set up Git hooks or use third-party GitHub Actions for CI/CD

auto-deploy.comment: No native auto-deploy on push. Users can configure Git hooks or CI services (GitHub Actions, Travis CI, CircleCI) to trigger surge CLI deploys

cli-deployment.comment: CLI deployment is Surge's primary and defining feature: run 'surge' in your project directory to publish

build-system.comment: Surge has no build pipeline; it uploads pre-built static files only. Users must build locally or in CI before deploying

supported-frameworks.comment: Surge serves any static HTML/CSS/JS output. No framework auto-detection or special framework support; works with any static site generator's output

monorepo-support.comment: No build system means no monorepo configuration. Users can point the CLI to any subdirectory manually

deploy-previews.comment: No built-in deploy preview feature. Third-party GitHub Actions exist (e.g., surge-pr-preview) that deploy to unique surge.sh subdomains per PR

instant-rollbacks.comment: No rollback feature. To revert, users must re-deploy a previous build via the CLI

global-cdn.comment: Surge operates its own CDN with 8 regions globally and geo-aware DNS routing via geo.surge.world

cdn-provider.comment: Surge built its own CDN rather than using an existing provider. Uses distributed edge nodes with geo-routing

edge-locations.comment: 8 regions globally, including endpoints like sgp (Singapore) and na-west1 (North America West). Expanding over time

http2.comment: HTTP/2 has been requested since 2016 but remains unimplemented as of 2026. The GitHub issue is still open with no resolution

http3.comment: HTTP/3 is not supported; HTTP/2 is not even implemented yet

response-headers.comment: No support for custom response headers. CORS is configured via a CORS file (Pro only), but arbitrary headers (e.g., HSTS) are not supported

image-optimization.comment: No built-in image optimization or transformation features

asset-compression.comment: Automatic Gzip compression is applied by the Surge CDN. No Brotli support documented

custom-domains.comment: Custom domains are supported on the free tier via CNAME records

free-ssl.comment: Basic SSL is free for surge.sh subdomains. Custom domain SSL requires Surge Professional ($30/month)

wildcard-ssl.comment: Wildcard SSL certificates are supported on Surge Professional; users upload their own certificate (e.g., *.example.com)

auto-https-redirect.comment: Requires Surge Professional. Force HTTP to HTTPS redirect is a paid feature

custom-domain-preview.comment: No built-in preview deployment system, so no custom domain per preview

serverless-functions.comment: Surge is purely a static file hosting service with no server-side execution capabilities

scheduled-functions.comment: No scheduled function or cron support

authentication.comment: No built-in user authentication service. Password protection (HTTP basic auth) exists but is for site-level access, not user identity management

redirects.comment: Requires Surge Professional. Configured via a ROUTER file supporting 301 and 307 redirects with pattern matching

rewrites.comment: Surge supports redirects via ROUTER file but not transparent URL rewrites or reverse proxying

custom-404.comment: Place a 404.html file in your project root. Available on all plans including free

spa-fallback.comment: Place a 200.html file in the project root to serve as a fallback for client-side routing. Available on all plans

headers-config.comment: No support for configuring custom response headers per-path or globally. CORS is handled separately via a CORS file (Pro only)

ab-testing.comment: No A/B testing or traffic splitting features

free-team-members.comment: Collaborators can be added via CLI with no documented limit. Each collaborator gets publish access to the domain

rbac.comment: Collaborators all get the same publish access. No differentiated roles or permission levels

environment-variables.comment: Surge has no build system and does not support environment variables for builds. SURGE_LOGIN and SURGE_TOKEN are used for CI authentication only

preview-comments.comment: No preview comment feature (no preview deployment system exists)

ddos-protection.comment: Surge's privacy policy mentions IP-based mitigation for DDoS and abuse, but no specific DDoS protection feature is documented or marketed

soc2.comment: No SOC 2 certification documented or claimed

gdpr.comment: Surge has a privacy policy but no documented GDPR compliance measures or certification

password-protection.comment: Requires Surge Professional. Configured via an AUTH file with HTTP Basic Authentication. Supports multiple username/password pairs

documentation-quality.comment: Documentation covers basics (getting started, custom domains, 404/200 pages) but is sparse, with short pages, no search, limited advanced topics, and some content appears outdated

integrations.comment: No official first-party integrations with analytics, CMS, monitoring, or other services

community.comment: GitHub issues/discussions are the primary community channel. Some Stack Overflow presence. No Discord or forum

status-page.comment: Public status page at surge.statuspage.io with uptime history and incident notifications
